groundsForAppeal: The Commission failed to consider the comprehensive technical and financial documentation submitted with the application. The rejection was based on incomplete information and did not give the applicant opportunity to address concerns.
factualBasis: Application EXP/2023/156 was submitted on 15 November 2023 with complete documentation including geological surveys, financial statements, and environmental plans. No request for additional information was made before rejection.
reliefSought: Set aside the decision and grant exploration licence EXP/2023/156, or remit the matter for reconsideration with proper evaluation of submitted documentation.
hearingPanel: ["Hon. Joseph Mwape (Chairperson)", "Mr. David Phiri", "Mrs. Grace Lungu"]

timeline.description: Notice of appeal filed by Copperbelt Mining Ltd
All parties served with appeal documents
Hearing scheduled for 15 March 2024 at 10:00 AM

groundsForAppeal: The compliance order is unreasonable as it requires actions within 30 days that are technically impossible to complete. The order demonstrates lack of understanding of mining operations.
factualBasis: Compliance order CO/2024/023 requires installation of water treatment facility within 30 days. Industry standard for such installation is 6-12 months including procurement, construction, and commissioning.
reliefSought: Vary the compliance order to allow reasonable timeframe of 9 months for installation of water treatment facility.
hearingPanel: ["Hon. Joseph Mwape (Chairperson)", "Eng. Patrick Tembo", "Mrs. Grace Lungu"]

timeline.description: Notice of appeal filed by Northern Gems Ltd
All parties served with appeal documents

groundsForAppeal: Suspension order was issued without proper investigation and without giving the licence holder opportunity to be heard. Alleged environmental violations were not substantiated.
factualBasis: Suspension order SUSP/2023/089 was based on single inspection report dated 10 October 2023. No follow-up investigation conducted and no hearing granted before suspension.
reliefSought: Set aside the suspension order and restore mining licence ML/2021/234 with immediate effect.
hearingPanel: ["Hon. Joseph Mwape (Chairperson)", "Mr. David Phiri", "Dr. Ruth Sakala"]
hearingNotes: Hearing conducted. Evidence heard from both parties. Commission presented inspection report. Appellant presented compliance certificates and corrective action evidence.
evidenceSubmitted: ["Inspection Report 2023/089", "ZEMA Compliance Certificate", "Corrective Action Report", "Expert Environmental Assessment"]
witnessStatements: ["Inspector John Banda", "Environmental Officer Mary Chanda", "Mine Manager Robert Mulenga"]
tribunalDecisionRemarks: The Tribunal finds that while there were some procedural irregularities in the suspension process, environmental compliance issues were substantiated. Appeal partially allowed. Suspension lifted conditional on completion of remediation works within 90 days.
tribunalOrderDetails: Suspension order set aside. Mining licence ML/2021/234 restored subject to conditions: (1) Complete remediation of identified environmental issues within 90 days; (2) Submit monthly compliance reports to MRC for 6 months; (3) Engage independent environmental auditor.
costsJustification: Appellant succeeded on procedural grounds. Costs awarded to reflect partial success.

timeline.description: Notice of appeal filed by Zambian Gold Explorers
All parties served with appeal documents
Main hearing conducted. Evidence and submissions heard from both parties.
Tribunal decision issued. Appeal partially allowed.

groundsForAppeal: Certificate was rejected on grounds of alleged previous violations which were never proven or adjudicated. Decision violates principles of natural justice.
factualBasis: Application for gold panning certificate GPC/2024/012 was rejected citing previous violations in 2021. No formal proceedings were ever instituted for alleged violations and applicant was never convicted.
reliefSought: Set aside rejection and grant gold panning certificate for designated area in Kabwe District.

timeline.description: Notice of appeal filed by Peter Mwale
All parties served with appeal documents

groundsForAppeal: Assessment of annual area charge is based on incorrect area calculation. Commission included areas that were relinquished in previous year.
factualBasis: Annual area charge assessment AAC/2023/156 calculated charge on 15,000 hectares. Appellant relinquished 3,000 hectares on 15 January 2023 as evidenced by formal relinquishment notice REL/2023/045.
reliefSought: Vary the assessment to reflect correct area of 12,000 hectares and recalculate annual area charge accordingly.
hearingPanel: ["Hon. Joseph Mwape (Chairperson)", "Surveyor George Banda", "Mrs. Grace Lungu"]
hearingNotes: Documentary hearing. Cadastral records reviewed. Commission acknowledged administrative error in area calculation.
evidenceSubmitted: ["Relinquishment Notice REL/2023/045", "Cadastral Maps", "Payment Records", "Licence Register Extract"]
tribunalDecisionRemarks: The Tribunal finds that the appellant's relinquishment of 3,000 hectares was properly registered and should have been reflected in the annual area charge calculation. Appeal allowed in full.
tribunalOrderDetails: Annual area charge assessment AAC/2023/156 set aside. Commission directed to issue revised assessment based on 12,000 hectares. Overpayment of K45,000 to be refunded or credited to appellant's account within 30 days.
costsJustification: Appeal succeeded due to Commission's administrative error. Costs awarded to appellant.

timeline.description: Notice of appeal filed by Luapula Mining Corporation
All parties served with appeal documents
Documentary hearing conducted. Cadastral records reviewed.
Tribunal decision issued. Appeal allowed in full.
